structured_payload_candidate.title: Constant Value Thresholds for Airflow Alarms
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Series fan-powered terminal unit, variable-volume fan
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The constant value thresholds for airflow sensor calibration and leaking damper alarms are a function of the transducer and A/D converter; the value used should be determined as the minimum accuracy of the combination.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: The constant value thresholds for the airflow sensor calibration and leaking damper alarms are a function of the transducer and A/D converter used to measure airflow. The value used should be determined as the minimum accuracy of the transducer and A/D converter combination.

evidence_quote: The constant value thresholds for the airflow sensor calibration and leaking damper alarms are a function of the transducer and A/D converter used to measure airflow. The value used should be determined as the minimum accuracy of the transducer and A/D converter combination.
evidence_text: The constant value thresholds for the airflow sensor calibration and leaking damper alarms are a function of the transducer and A/D converter used to measure airflow. The value used should be determined as the minimum accuracy of the transducer and A/D converter combination.
judge_reason: Guidance on constant value thresholds for airflow alarms

structured_payload_candidate.title: Heating Hot-Water Plant Start/Stop Logic
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Series fan-powered terminal unit, variable-volume fan
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Typically, the heating hot-water plant will start when there is at least one request for 5 minutes, and stop when there are no requests for 5 minutes, after a minimum run-time has elapsed.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: Typically, the heating hot-water plant will start when there is at least one request for 5 minutes, and stop when there are no requests for 5 minutes, after a minimum run-time has elapsed.

evidence_quote: Typically, the heating hot-water plant will start when there is at least one request for 5 minutes, and stop when there are no requests for 5 minutes, after a minimum run-time has elapsed.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Standard 90.1 Overhead Supply Air Temperature Limit
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Series fan-powered terminal unit, variable-volume fan
structured_payload_candidate.summary: ASHRAE/IES Standard 90.1-2016 limits overhead supply air to 11°C (20°F) above space temperature to minimize stratification.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: ASHRAE/IES Standard 90.1-2016 limits overhead supply air to 11°C (20°F) above space temperature (e.g., 32°C [90°F] at 21°C [70°F] space temperature setpoint) to minimize stratification.

evidence_quote: ASHRAE/IES Standard 90.1-2016 limits overhead supply air to 11°C (20°F) above space temperature (e.g., 32°C [90°F] at 21°C [70°F] space temperature setpoint) to minimize stratification.
evidence_text: ASHRAE/IES Standard 90.1-2016 limits overhead supply air to 11°C (20°F) above space temperature (e.g., 32°C [90°F] at 21°C [70°F] space temperature setpoint) to minimize stratification.
judge_reason: Application guidance citing Standard 90.1 limit, grounded in Guideline 36 context

structured_payload_candidate.title: Endpoints as Function of Zone Group Mode

Table 5.10.4 Endpoints as a Function of Zone Group Mode

Minimum  Vmin*  0 0 0 0 0

Minimum  Vmin*  0 0 0 0 0
evidence_text:

structured_payload_candidate.title: Cooling Zone State - Primary Airflow Setpoint Mapping
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: When the Zone State Is Cooling
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Series fan-powered terminal unit, variable-volume fan
structured_payload_candidate.summary: In cooling mode, the cooling loop output is mapped to the active primary airflow setpoint from the minimum endpoint to the cooling maximum endpoint. If supply air temperature from the air handler is greater than room temperature, the active primary airflow setpoint shall be no higher than the minimum endpoint and the series fan airflow setpoint shall be no higher than OA-min.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Map cooling loop output to active primary airflow setpoint from minimum endpoint to cooling maximum endpoint. If AHU SAT > room temperature, set primary airflow setpoint ≤ minimum endpoint and series fan airflow setpoint ≤ OA-min.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: a. The cooling-loop output shall be mapped to the active primary airflow setpoint from the minimum endpoint to the cooling maximum endpoint. 1. If supply air temperature from the air handler is greater than room temperature, the active primary airflow setpoint shall be no higher than the minimum endpoint and the series fan airflow setpoint shall be no higher than OA-min.

evidence_quote: a. The cooling-loop output shall be mapped to the active primary airflow setpoint from the minimum endpoint to the cooling maximum endpoint. 1. If supply air temperature from the air handler is greater than room temperature, the active primary airflow setpoint shall be no higher than the minimum endpoint and the series fan airflow setpoint shall be no higher than OA-min.
evidence_text: a. The cooling-loop output shall be mapped to the active primary airflow setpoint from the minimum endpoint to the cooling maximum endpoint. 1. If supply air temperature from the air handler is greater than room temperature, the active primary airflow setpoint shall be no higher than the minimum endpoint and the series fan airflow setpoint shall be no higher than OA-min.
judge_reason: Operational rule for primary airflow setpoint mapping in cooling mode

structured_payload_candidate.title: Cooling Zone State - Series Fan Airflow Setpoint Mapping
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: When the Zone State Is Cooling
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Series fan-powered terminal unit, variable-volume fan
structured_payload_candidate.summary: In cooling mode, the cooling loop output is mapped to the series fan airflow setpoint from the larger of OA-min and the primary airflow minimum endpoint to the cooling maximum endpoint.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Map cooling loop output to series fan airflow setpoint from max(OA-min, primary airflow minimum endpoint) to cooling maximum endpoint.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: b. The Cooling Loop output shall be mapped to the series fan airflow setpoint from the larger of OA-min and the primary airflow minimum endpoint to the cooling maximum endpoint.

evidence_quote: b. The Cooling Loop output shall be mapped to the series fan airflow setpoint from the larger of OA-min and the primary airflow minimum endpoint to the cooling maximum endpoint.
evidence_text: b. The Cooling Loop output shall be mapped to the series fan airflow setpoint from the larger of OA-min and the primary airflow minimum endpoint to the cooling maximum endpoint.
judge_reason: Operational rule for series fan airflow setpoint mapping in cooling mode

structured_payload_candidate.title: Deadband Zone State - Series Fan Airflow Setpoint
structured_payload_candidate.section_title: When the Zone State Is Deadband
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Series fan-powered terminal unit, variable-volume fan
structured_payload_candidate.summary: In deadband mode, the series fan airflow setpoint is set to OA-min.
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Set series fan airflow setpoint equal to OA-min.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: b. The series fan airflow setpoint shall be equal to OA-min.

evidence_quote: b. The series fan airflow setpoint shall be equal to OA-min.
evidence_text: b. The series fan airflow setpoint shall be equal to OA-min.
judge_reason: Deadband series fan airflow setpoint rule

structured_payload_candidate.title: Heating Zone State - Second Stage (50-100% Heating Loop)
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Series fan-powered terminal unit, variable-volume fan
structured_payload_candidate.summary: From 50% to 100% heating loop output, reset the series fan airflow setpoint from OA-min to Sfan-htgmax. Primary airflow setpoint remains minimum endpoint.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Zone State is Heating and Heating Loop output is between 50% and 100%
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Reset series fan airflow setpoint from OA-min to Sfan-htgmax. Active primary airflow setpoint = minimum endpoint.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: b. From 50% to 100%, the Heating Loop output shall reset the series fan airflow setpoint from OA-min to a Sfan-htgmax. The active primary airflow setpoint shall be the minimum endpoint.

evidence_quote: b. From 50% to 100%, the Heating Loop output shall reset the series fan airflow setpoint from OA-min to a Sfan-htgmax. The active primary airflow setpoint shall be the minimum endpoint.
evidence_text: b. From 50% to 100%, the Heating Loop output shall reset the series fan airflow setpoint from OA-min to a Sfan-htgmax. The active primary airflow setpoint shall be the minimum endpoint.
judge_reason: Heating second stage fan airflow reset sequence

structured_payload_candidate.title: VAV Damper Modulation
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Series fan-powered terminal unit, variable-volume fan
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The VAV damper is modulated to maintain the measured airflow at setpoint.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Always active when airflow setpoint is non-zero
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Modulate VAV damper to maintain measured airflow at setpoint.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: 5.10.5.4. The VAV damper shall be modulated to maintain the measured airflow at setpoint.

evidence_quote: 5.10.5.4. The VAV damper shall be modulated to maintain the measured airflow at setpoint.
evidence_text: 5.10.5.4. The VAV damper shall be modulated to maintain the measured airflow at setpoint.
judge_reason: Standard VAV damper modulation control

structured_payload_candidate.title: Fan Control - Run Conditions and Start Sequence
structured_payload_candidate.equipment_scope: Series fan-powered terminal unit, variable-volume fan
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Fan runs whenever zone is in heating or cooling Zone State, or if the associated Zone Group is in Occupied Mode. Prior to starting the fan, the damper is driven fully closed to prevent backward rotation. After a fixed time delay (15 seconds) with fan proven ON, the damper override is released.
structured_payload_candidate.trigger_condition: Zone State is Heating or Cooling, or Zone Group is in Occupied Mode
structured_payload_candidate.required_behavior: Run fan. Before starting, drive damper fully closed. After fan proven ON for 15 seconds, release damper override.
structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: 5.10.5.5. Fan Control. Fan shall run whenever zone is in heating or cooling Zone State, or if the associated Zone Group is in Occupied Mode. Prior to starting the fan, the damper is first driven fully closed to ensure that the fan is not rotating backward. Once the fan is proven ON for a fixed time delay (15 seconds), the damper override is released.

evidence_quote: 5.10.5.5. Fan Control. Fan shall run whenever zone is in heating or cooling Zone State, or if the associated Zone Group is in Occupied Mode. Prior to starting the fan, the damper is first driven fully closed to ensure that the fan is not rotating backward. Once the fan is proven ON for a fixed time delay (15 seconds), the damper override is released.
evidence_text: 5.10.5.5. Fan Control. Fan shall run whenever zone is in heating or cooling Zone State, or if the associated Zone Group is in Occupied Mode. Prior to starting the fan, the damper is first driven fully closed to ensure that the fan is not rotating backward. Once the fan is proven ON for a fixed time delay (15 seconds), the damper override is released.
judge_reason: Fan start/stop logic and start sequence

structured_payload_candidate.title: Leaking Damper Alarm

structured_payload_candidate.evidence_quote: 5.10.6.5. Leaking Damper. If the damper position is 0%, and airflow sensor reading is above the larger of 10% of the cooling maximum airflow setpoint or 50 cfm for 10 minutes while the fan serving the zone is proven ON, generate a Level 4 alarm.

evidence_quote: 5.10.6.5. Leaking Damper. If the damper position is 0%, and airflow sensor reading is above the larger of 10% of the cooling maximum airflow setpoint or 50 cfm for 10 minutes while the fan serving the zone is proven ON, generate a Level 4 alarm.
evidence_text: 5.10.6.5. Leaking Damper. If the damper position is 0%, and airflow sensor reading is above the larger of 10% of the cooling maximum airflow setpoint or 50 cfm for 10 minutes while the fan serving the zone is proven ON, generate a Level 4 alarm.
